summary: Camera-focused decomp PR that replaced many camera.c asm stubs or UNK prototypes with C implementations and refined camera-related structs, bitfields, constants, and headers. The decomp.dev bot reported +1340 matched bytes, 6 new 100% matches, and 7 partial function improvements. There was no substantive human review text in the slice, so intent beyond the diff and match report is inferred only from changed code.

key_files.role: Main camera decomp work; added implementations for multiple Camera_* functions and updated helper logic.
Refined camera data structures, including CameraQuake, Camera bitfields, offsets, Vec3 fields, callback union, and callback function pointer typing.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and void-only prototypes with typed signatures for newly understood camera functions.
Corrected several camera constants from float arrays to scalar f64 constants with pi-derived meanings and thresholds.

what_changed: Implemented or partially implemented camera mode application in Camera_8002A4AC, including mode-specific HSD_CObjSetFov, HSD_CObjSetInterest, HSD_CObjSetEyePosition, near/far plane handling, debug camera modes 7 and 8, and stage-specific floor clamping for some internal stage IDs.

Added Camera_8002B0E0 logic using gm_8016B41C single-player detection, Player_GetPlayerId, HSD_PadCopyStatus substick Y input, and cm_803BCCA0 tuning values to update and clamp camera state fields unk_2ba and unk_2bc.
Added vector/math-oriented implementations for Camera_8002BAA8 and Camera_8002BC78, including explicit reciprocal square root refinement, lbVector_Normalize, and PSVECCrossProduct usage.
Added Camera_8002EA64 and Camera_8002FE38 behavior for camera mode 6 target state, replacing commented offset-by-offset assignments with struct/Vec3 assignments and grouped bitfields.

Changed fn_8002F360 and CameraModeCallbacks so camera mode callbacks accept a void* argument, and implemented Camera_8002F3AC to invoke the current mode callback and copy target movement values to active movement values.

Implemented Camera_8002F7AC camera mode setup for a player slot, using Player_GetEntity, ftLib_80086B74 camera box direction, HSD_Randi/HSD_Randf, Stage_GetCamAngleRadiansLeft/Right, and pitch/yaw offsets.

Added Camera_800307D0 frustum/edge projection logic using HSD_CObjGetAspect, HSD_CObjGetFov, HSD_CObjGetEyePosition, HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, sinf/cosf, threshold constants, and large sentinel return values.
Implemented Camera_SetStageVisible by setting cm_80452C68.unk_399_b5 based on whether the argument is zero.
Implemented Camera_80030BBC as a world-to-screen helper using lbVector_WorldToScreen, screen coordinate range checks, optional S32Vec2 output, and HSD_CObjGetScissor bounds tests.
Implemented Camera_80030CFC subject-on-screen logic using camera eye/interest, CameraBox position/size, lbVector_8000E838, lbVector_Diff, lbVector_Normalize, lbVector_Add, and Camera_80030BBC.

Renamed/clarified camera struct fields: UnkInternalCameraStruct became CameraQuake with Vec3 x0 and int type; camera quake arrays use CameraQuake; unk_2C8/unk_2CC became pitch_offset/yaw_offset; CameraBox fields gained comments for position, direction, and size.
Expanded Camera struct layout around offsets 0x2F8 through 0x378 with Vec3 fields, scalar fields, grouped bitfields for unk_341, and a union at unk_35C containing either Vec3 vec or s32 (*cb)(f32*).

Updated camera.static.h constants cm_804D7EC8, cm_804D7ED0, cm_804D7ED8, cm_804D7EE0, and cm_804D7EE8 from odd float arrays to scalar f64 constants corresponding to M_PI_8, M_PI_16, 2*pi, pi, and 0.0001.
smart_moves: Used typed function signatures in camera.h to lock down known arguments and returns instead of leaving new progress behind UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Converted repeated raw component assignments in Camera_8002FE38 into whole-Vec3 assignments, which both clarifies intent and can match when the compiler emits struct copies.
Grouped adjacent camera flag bits as 2-bit bitfields where code treated them as small mode values rather than independent booleans.
Reinterpreted the old 4-s32 quake structure as Vec3 plus type, which enabled meaningful use of .type instead of xC and better reflected Camera_8002A28C behavior.
Identified pi-derived static constants and replaced misleading float-array declarations with scalar f64 constants, making random pitch/yaw code in Camera_8002F7AC much more readable.
Used existing engine APIs rather than hand-written math where applicable: CObj getters/setters, lbVector helpers, PSVECCrossProduct, HSD_Randf/HSD_Randi, and stage camera-angle accessors.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK calls, literal sentinel floats, and explicit __frsqrte refinement where the generated code likely depends on them.
decomp_lessons: For camera code, understanding the Camera struct layout often unlocks multiple functions at once; this PR paired function decomp with field renames and offset expansion.
When a field stores a camera vector, prefer Vec3 struct fields and whole-struct assignments over separate x/y/z placeholder floats if the code and offsets support it.
Callback arrays should be typed to match call sites. Changing CameraModeCallbacks.callback from void* to void (*)(void*) allowed fn_8002F360 and Camera_8002F3AC to be represented directly.
Pi-related constants may appear as strange float arrays before relocation/type cleanup; compare usage sites for pitch/yaw/random-angle code before accepting odd array declarations.
Large sentinel float constants such as ±8.5070587e37f and float-int range guards can be important for camera projection and screen tests; keep exact literals when matching.
Stage-specific camera behavior may require direct internal_stage_id switches and calls to stage module helpers rather than generic ground queries.
Partial function progress is still useful when it clarifies headers and structs; the bot reported several functions improved substantially without reaching 100%.
naming_conventions: Camera quake data was promoted from UnkInternalCameraStruct to CameraQuake.
The quake field xC was renamed to type, evidenced by cm_80452C68.unk_B0[i][j].type = 0.
Camera pitch/yaw fields were renamed from unk_2C8 and unk_2CC to pitch_offset and yaw_offset.
Newly suspected CameraBox semantics were documented with comments: x1C as position, x28 as direction, and x34 as size.
Functions with known common names kept address-style symbols plus comments where known, such as Camera_SetModeTraining, Camera_ToScreen, and Camera_IsSubjectOnScreen.
Unknown camera mode state around unk_341 was represented as grouped bitfields named unk_341_b1_b2, unk_341_b3_b4, unk_341_b5_b6, and unk_341_b7 rather than separate one-bit flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK was inserted in several functions, including Camera_8002A4AC, Camera_8002EA64, Camera_8002F3AC, Camera_80030BBC, and Camera_80030CFC, suggesting stack-shape tuning for matching.
Camera_8002BAA8 used explicit __frsqrte and Newton-style refinement rather than only lbVector_Normalize, likely to match the original square-root sequence.
Camera_800307D0 retained exact threshold and sentinel constants, including cm_804D7EE8, cm_804D7EF0, 8.5070587e37f, and -8.5070587e37f.
Function pointer dispatch was written in a direct indexed form, cm_803BCB18.callback[cm_80452C68.mode](...), matching the table-driven camera mode design.
Whole-struct Vec3 copies were used for camera movement targets and state, which can produce closer codegen than manual component copies.
Randomized camera angle code used f64 temporaries/constants and HSD_Randf/HSD_Randi calls in a way consistent with the static constant type corrections.
Header prototype corrections removed UNK signatures for functions where argument and return types were established, helping later translation units compile with accurate calls.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased to 32.66%, +0.03%, +1340 bytes.
Reported 6 new 100% matches: Camera_8002F7AC (+328), Camera_80030BBC (+284), Camera_8002BC78 (+272), Camera_8002F3AC (+200), Camera_8002FE38 (+148), and fn_8002F360 (+38).

Reported 7 unmatched-function improvements: Camera_8002A4AC to 78.29%, Camera_800307D0 to 73.12%, Camera_8002BAA8 to 87.07%, Camera_8002EA64 to 88.89%, Camera_80030CFC to 82.76%, Camera_8002B0E0 to 52.86%, and Camera_SetStageVisible to 77.78%.
No human review comments or reviewer suggestions were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching Camera_8002A4AC, Camera_800307D0, Camera_8002BAA8, Camera_8002EA64, Camera_80030CFC, Camera_8002B0E0, or Camera_SetStageVisible to see how the partial matches were completed.
Search for subsequent renames of CameraBox x1C, x28, and x34 to confirm whether position, direction, and size comments became established names.
Search for uses of CameraQuake and unk_B0/unk_1B0 to determine whether CameraQuake fields x0 and type were later refined.
Check whether the project later replaced cm_804D7EC8/cm_804D7ED0/cm_804D7ED8/cm_804D7EE0 with named M_PI-derived constants.
Compare callback users after this PR to confirm the void* CameraModeCallbacks signature remained correct across all camera modes.
